Title: Pangenome analyses of tea plants reveal structural variations driving gene expression alterations and agronomic trait diversification

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Wild tea relative (CT); cultivated tea plants (CSA/CSS)
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Camellia taliensis; Camellia sinensis var. assamica; Camellia sinensis var. sinensis
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: CT accessions and cultivated tea accessions; VIGS performed in Longjin43 for CsLRR1
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CT1, CT4, CT7, YK11, YK47, FDDB, ZYQ; Longjin43 for VIGS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CtLRR1 is significantly upregulated after Colletotrichum gloeosporioides infection in CT accessions; CT carries a 159 bp promoter insertion
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in infected CT than in cultivated tea plants; silencing in cultivated tea reduces transcript levels
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher LRR1 expression compromises resistance to Colletotrichum gloeosporioides; silencing enhances resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathogen infection phenotyping; transcriptome DEG analysis; promoter SV identification; PCR genotyping; dual-luciferase promoter assay; VIGS knockdown; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LRR1 was the sole LRR-domain DEG with promoter SV support among pathogen-responsive genes associated with CT susceptibility. 2. A CT-specific 159 bp promoter insertion increases promoter activity and elevates CtLRR1 expression. 3. Higher LRR1 expression is negatively correlated with resistance to C. gloeosporioides. 4. VIGS silencing of CsLRR1 reduces lesion size, indicating LRR1 acts as a negative regulator of defense.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native LRR1 promoters from CT, CSA, CSS; proCtLRR1, proCsaLRR1, proCssLRR1 in transient assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of pathogen-treated accessions (NGDC PRJCA036376); resequencing/SV genotyping (PRJCA036909); genome assemblies (PRJCA050339); Hi-C (PRJCA048375)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated LRR1 expression in wild relatives is associated with increased pathogen susceptibility
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid/highlight the CT-specific 159 bp promoter insertion or reduce LRR1 expression to improve anthracnose resistance in tea breeding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper classifies LRR1 as an LRR-domain-containing protein and a negative regulator of immune response; sequence variation in UTR/CDS was not correlated with function, implicating promoter SV as causal.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Wild tea relative (CT); cultivated tea plants (CSA/CSS)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Camellia taliensis; Camellia sinensis var. assamica; Camellia sinensis var. sinensis
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DLC and other CT accessions; CSA and CSS accessions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CT, CSA, CSS accessions; transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in CT accessions than in cultivated tea plants; significantly elevated in wild relatives carrying the 192 bp promoter insertion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in purple-shoot wild relatives; lower in cultivated green-shoot tea plants
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Anthocyanidins including delphinidin and cyanidin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to purple shoot coloration and tea quality-related pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ANS3 expression promotes anthocyanin accumulation; CT-specific promoter insertion is associated with elevated anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population SV analysis; qRT-PCR; HPLC/UPLC metabolite quantification; correlation analysis; promoter activity dual-luciferase assay; promoter deletion assay; PCR genotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS3 was identified within domestication-associated SV regions and is expressed significantly more highly in CT than in cultivated tea plants. 2. A CT-associated 192 bp promoter insertion (Hap1) increases promoter activity and ANS3 transcript abundance. 3. Higher ANS3 expression positively correlates with anthocyanin accumulation, including delphinidin and cyanidin. 4. Deletion of the 192 bp fragment reduces promoter activity, supporting a causal role of the promoter SV.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tea domestication background; comparison among CT, CSA, and CSS populations
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ANS3 promoters from CT, CSA, CSS; proCtANS3, proCsaANS3, proCssANS3; truncated proCtANS3-192 in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NGDC PRJCA036376); resequencing/SV genotyping (PRJCA036909); metabolite profiling of anthocyanins; genome assemblies (PRJCA050339); Hi-C (PRJCA048375)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; paper supports promoter SV-mediated transcriptional control of a key late anthocyanin enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: The ancestral 192 bp insertion and higher anthocyanin accumulation were largely lost during domestication; no explicit growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/target to increase anthocyanin-rich purple-shoot tea by retaining or introducing the CT-associated 192 bp promoter insertion or high-activity promoter haplotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The 283 bp promoter insertion in CSS is structurally unrelated to the 192 bp CT insertion and did not significantly affect ANS3 expression, anthocyanin content, or promoter activity.
